title: Download motor configuration from a matching motor and upload it to the replacement motor
summary: Use the Teknic ClearPath application and a USB connection to save a motor configuration from the motor being replaced or, if that file is unavailable, from a working motor in the same position. Save the configuration as an .mtr file, disconnect from the source motor, and upload the saved configuration to the replacement motor. If the replacement motor will be tested, ensure it is clamped down before testing.
when_to_use: Use during motor replacement when the replacement motor needs the correct configuration parameters transferred from the motor being replaced or from a working motor in the same position. The source notes that motors in the same position, such as Z1 or Z2, share the same parameters.
not_for: Do not use as a complete motor replacement procedure; the source section only provides the configuration transfer portion.
Do not use when detailed upload steps are required beyond the instruction to upload the saved configuration file; the source does not provide those detailed steps.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Connect to the motor, run Teknic ClearPath, save the .mtr configuration file, disconnect from the source motor, upload the saved configuration to the replacement motor, and ensure the replacement motor is clamped down before any testing.

safety_notes: If the new motor will be tested, make sure it is clamped down before testing.
This procedure is associated with motor replacement activity; the source packet does not define full safety boundaries for replacement or testing.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the motor USB connection
Configuration file saved as an .mtr file

access_required: Physical access to the motor USB connection point
Access to a laptop that can run the Teknic ClearPath application
Access to the replacement motor for configuration upload
system_preconditions: A matching motor position is identified if the original motor configuration file is not available.
The Teknic ClearPath application is available on the laptop.
A USB connection can be made between the motor and the laptop.
external_references: ClearPath User Manual for detailed upload steps if needed

visual_references.description: Page 122 maintenance diagram associated with motor replacement and likely showing the motor USB connection point or USB port cover used during configuration transfer.
Figure 7-5 identifying the Teknic SD Motor referenced in the motor replacement and software parameter download workflow.

steps.title: Identify a matching source motor if the original configuration file is unavailable
steps.purpose: Ensure the replacement motor receives parameters from a motor with the same position-specific configuration.
steps.instruction: If the configuration file for the motor being replaced is not available, identify a working motor in the same position as the replacement target, such as another Z1 motor for a Z1 replacement or another Z2 motor for a Z2 replacement.
steps.expected_result: A valid source motor in the same position is identified for configuration download.
steps.healthy_condition: A working motor in the same position is available and selected as the source of the configuration file.
steps.failure_condition: No matching-position working motor is identified, or the technician cannot confirm that the source motor is in the same position as the replacement target.

actions.label: Identify matching motor position
actions.exact_action: Identify a working motor in the same position as the replacement target if the original configuration file is not available.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the Teknic SD Motor figure to confirm the motor type associated with this configuration transfer workflow.

stop_or_escalate_if: A matching-position motor cannot be identified.
It is unclear whether the candidate source motor shares the same parameters as the replacement target.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you do not have the configuration file for the motor being replaced, download the configuration file from a working motor in the same position.

steps.title: Connect the motor to the laptop by USB
steps.purpose: Establish the physical connection needed for Teknic ClearPath to access the motor configuration.
steps.instruction: Connect the USB cable to the motor and the laptop.
steps.expected_result: The motor and laptop are physically connected by USB.
steps.healthy_condition: The USB cable is connected at both the motor and laptop connection points.
steps.failure_condition: The USB cable cannot be connected, the motor USB access point cannot be located, or the connection is not secure.

actions.label: Connect USB cable
actions.exact_action: Connect the USB cable to the motor and laptop.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the motor USB connection point and any labeled USB port cover associated with the motor replacement workflow.
Use the Teknic SD Motor figure to identify the motor referenced by the procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The USB connection point cannot be located.
The USB cable cannot be connected to the motor and laptop.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Connect the USB cable to the motor and laptop.
Maintenance diagram on page 122 associated with motor replacement and removal, likely highlighting the motor USB connection point used for configuration transfer.

steps.title: Launch the Teknic ClearPath application
steps.purpose: Open the manufacturer software used to read and save the motor configuration.
steps.instruction: Run the Teknic ClearPath application on the laptop.
steps.expected_result: The Teknic ClearPath application is open and available for configuration operations.
steps.healthy_condition: The application launches successfully on the laptop.
steps.failure_condition: The application is unavailable or does not launch.

actions.label: Open ClearPath application
actions.exact_action: Run the Teknic ClearPath application on the laptop.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Teknic ClearPath application is not available on the laptop.

steps.title: Save the motor configuration as an .mtr file
steps.purpose: Create the configuration file needed for transfer to the replacement motor.
steps.instruction: From the File menu, select Save Configuration and save the configuration as an .mtr file.
steps.expected_result: An .mtr configuration file is saved from the source motor.
steps.healthy_condition: The configuration is saved successfully as an .mtr file and is available for later upload.
steps.failure_condition: The configuration file is not saved, the save operation cannot be completed, or the file is not available as an .mtr file.

actions.label: Save configuration file
actions.exact_action: From the File menu, select Save Configuration and save the configuration as an .mtr file.

stop_or_escalate_if: The File > Save Configuration action is unavailable.
The configuration cannot be saved as an .mtr file.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Use File > Save Configuration to save an .mtr file.

steps.title: Disconnect from the source motor
steps.purpose: End the connection to the source motor after the configuration file has been saved.
steps.instruction: Disconnect from the motor after saving the configuration file.
steps.expected_result: The source motor is disconnected after the configuration file is saved.
steps.healthy_condition: The saved configuration file is retained and the source motor is no longer connected.
steps.failure_condition: The motor remains connected unexpectedly or the saved file is not confirmed before disconnecting.

actions.label: Disconnect source motor
actions.exact_action: Disconnect from the motor after saving the configuration file.

stop_or_escalate_if: The configuration file was not successfully saved before disconnecting.
The connection cannot be cleanly ended.

steps.title: Upload the saved configuration to the replacement motor
steps.purpose: Transfer the saved parameters to the new motor so it uses the correct configuration.
steps.instruction: Upload the saved configuration file to the new motor.
steps.expected_result: The replacement motor receives the saved configuration file.
steps.healthy_condition: The saved .mtr file is available and used for upload to the replacement motor.
steps.failure_condition: The upload cannot be completed or detailed upload actions are needed but not provided in the source.

actions.label: Upload saved configuration
actions.exact_action: Upload the saved configuration file to the new motor.

stop_or_escalate_if: Detailed upload steps are required and are not available in this source.
The saved configuration file cannot be uploaded to the replacement motor.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Upload the configuration file to the new motor.

steps.title: Clamp the replacement motor before any testing
steps.purpose: Reduce risk during any post-upload motor testing.
steps.instruction: If the new motor will be tested, make sure it is clamped down before testing.
steps.expected_result: The replacement motor is secured before testing begins.
steps.healthy_condition: The motor is clamped down before any test is performed.
steps.failure_condition: Testing is planned or started without the motor being clamped down.

actions.label: Clamp motor before test
actions.exact_action: If the new motor will be tested, make sure it is clamped down before testing.

stop_or_escalate_if: The motor cannot be clamped down before testing.
Testing is requested without the motor being secured.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you plan on testing the motor, make sure it is clamped down.

success_criteria: A configuration file is saved as an .mtr file from the motor being replaced or from a working motor in the same position.
The saved configuration file is available for upload to the replacement motor.
The replacement motor receives the saved configuration.
If testing is performed, the replacement motor is clamped down before testing.
healthy_conditions: A matching-position source motor is identified when the original configuration file is unavailable.
The USB connection between the motor and laptop is established.
The configuration is saved as an .mtr file.
The saved file is available for upload to the replacement motor.
failure_conditions: No matching-position source motor can be identified.
The USB connection to the motor cannot be established.
Teknic ClearPath is unavailable or cannot be run.
The configuration cannot be saved as an .mtr file.
The source does not provide the detailed upload steps needed to complete the upload.
The motor is not clamped down before testing.
escalation_guidance: If detailed upload steps are needed, consult the ClearPath User Manual referenced by the source section.
Escalate when a matching-position source motor cannot be identified.
Escalate when the Teknic ClearPath application is unavailable or the configuration cannot be saved.
Use a higher-support role if site safety or service controls require it for motor replacement or testing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Motor USB connection point or USB port cover associated with the page 122 motor replacement workflow.
Teknic SD Motor identification relevant to the configuration transfer procedure.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: If you do not have the configuration file for the motor being replaced, download the configuration file from a working motor in the same position. Connect the USB cable to the motor and laptop, run the Teknic ClearPath application, use File > Save Configuration to save an .mtr file, disconnect from the motor, and upload the configuration file to the new motor. If you plan on testing the motor, make sure it is clamped down.

missing_details: The source does not provide detailed upload steps for transferring the saved configuration to the new motor.
The source does not provide a time estimate for this configuration transfer procedure.
The source does not define whether production stop or LOTO is required for this specific configuration transfer activity.
The source does not define explicit role boundaries beyond the inferred support level.
